observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to secure the list and supporting documents of recorded lots from the Municipal Assessor and verify their validity and existence to support their proper recognition in the Municipality’s books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue reconciling the PPE accounts reported in the Financial Statements and RPCPPE in coordination with the General Services Office, and make the necessary corrections and adjustments to address the noted discrepancies.

Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to finally establish accurate and reliable PPE balances.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
Closely coordinate to determine the causes of discrepancies between their records and implement the necessary entries to reconcile their books; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers/accounting records at the end of each month or as needed.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to implement the Perpetual Inventory Method in the accounting of inventories in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs Volume I;
GSO to ensure that the issuance of all supplies and materials are supported with Requisition and Issue Slips and that the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued is prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for the recording of expense; and
Municipal Accountant and the GSO to maintain the required Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and Stock Cards, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Agriculturist and other concerned personnel to exert effort to determine the status and nature of the recorded Biological asset, so that proper adjusting entries or derecognition from the FS, if warranted, can be made in accordance with the requirements of IPSAS 1 and 27 for the recognition of biological asset.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the implementing offices of the Municipality secure the MOA for each fund transfer and expedite the implementation of projects funded by transferred funds in accordance with the MOA and submit the necessary liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording in the books.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to determine the unexpended balances of fully implemented fund transfers and facilitate their return to the respective source agencies.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Analyze and determine the causes of variances between the subsidiary/general ledgers and bank confirmation and communicate with the bank about the matter, if necessary;
Reconcile noted discrepancies and provide the Audit Team with the necessary records and supporting documents to validate any adjustments made; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of loans payable with the bank to prevent future discrepancies between the SL/GL and the bank records.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
Acting-Municipal Treasurer to issue a check for the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from CYs 2023 and 2022 amounting to ₱5.705 million to LDRRMF-STF account in the Trust Fund; and
Municipal Accountant to issue a Journal Entry Voucher to record the transfer of the unexpended balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE from CYs 2023 and 2022 amounting to ₱5.705 million from the General Fund to the Trust Fund and to recognize Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the reconciliation statements on the comparison of budget and actual amounts as mandatory disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We reiterated our previous years' recommendation and the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office, agreed to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized by fully implementing the various programs, projects, and activities included in the AIP to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to:
a) Review the unimplemented PPAs charged against the current and continuing appropriations and determine whether such PPAs are still feasible, otherwise, reprogram it to implementable PPAs that would timely serve the purpose of the 20% DF; and
b) Ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation ready.
We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that the quarterly reports on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund are prepared and submitted to the concerned government agencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant and the Acting-Municipal Treasurer to revert to the National Treasury the unutilized balance of the Odette Fund amounting to ₱3,697.17;
Municipal Accountant and Acting-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that funds are available before processing the payment to the suppliers; and
MDRRMO to coordinate with the source agencies and donors of the financial assistance received by the Municipality during Typhoon Agaton regarding the utilization of the funds.

We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned Implementing Offices to expedite the implementation of programs/activities to utilize the fund transfers received within the prescribed period and strictly observe with the guidelines set in the Memoranda of Agreement and/or prescribed guidelines.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging regular expenses against the LDRRMF and prioritize the programs, projects, and activities that support disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery;
MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant to charge administrative expenses of the MDRRMO to the General Fund in the regular budget of the Municipality; and
MDRRMO, MSWDO, and Municipal Accountant to submit the status or inventory report, and distribution list of the 135 sacks of rice purchased for disaster preparedness program of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to establish a Petty Cash Fund (PCF) for the petty operating expenses of the Municipality and observe the guidelines in the handling and custody of PCF.

We likewise recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Disbursing Officer to stop the practice of drawing cash advances without specific purpose.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents, as enumerated in Annex J.
Concerned officers or employees to ensure that the Purchase Orders/Contracts were adequately supported; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are adequately supported with documents before processing the payments thereof, and adhere to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to follow the procedures mentioned in the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprise for the establishment and operation of proposed/new and existing LEEs.
We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to formulate a Business Plan on the proposed and existing LEEs and coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan in the enactment of an ordinance to support the legality of existence of the LEEs in accordance with the provisions of the Manual.
We likewise recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to consider the financial sustainability and viability of the existing LEE and ensure that the legal provisions governing LEE have been complied with before continuing operations.

The Municipality was not able to achieve its projected revenue for CY 2018 with a shortfall of P9,830,110.86; and revenue code and ordinances that have not been revised or updated since CY 1993 were used as basis for collections of taxes and fees, thereby casting doubts as to the propriety of taxes and fees imposed and the controls adopted in order to raise and collect additional revenue to support its programs, projects and activities that may benefit its constituents.

The Municipality was not compliant to the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage and did not fully maximize the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF), hence may result to insufficient capability to act and respond in times of disasters/emergencies and the Municipality was not able to implement the PPAs pertaining to disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery in the amount of P2,484,875.95, thereby exposing the Municipality to risks during and after a disaster/ calamity and depriving the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

The Municipality has not established its Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) nor allocated 1% of the Internal Revenue Allotment for the programs of the LCPC for CY 2017, contrary to Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120, thus deprived the children of the benefits that could be derived from the implementation of programs related to juvenile justice and welfare.

Disallowances which have become final and executory were not recorded under Receivables- Disallowances/Charges in the books of accounts, contrary to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-009, thus understated the receivable account.

The Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and the individual Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms of collectors and other accountable officers are not submitted by the Acting Municipal Treasurer to the Auditor contrary to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II, hence reconciliation of accountable forms received and issued could not be readily established.

The accuracy, completeness and existence of the reported balance of the Property Plant and Equipment (PPE) account amounting to ₱149.625 million as of                December 31, 2019 could not be ascertained due to the non-submission of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE, and the non-maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards contrary to pertinent provisions of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

The validity of the balance of Loans Payable - Domestic account amounting to ₱51.107 million could not be determined due to unreconciled balances between the General Ledger (GL) and the result of confirmation, as well as non-disclosure of the current and non-current portions of the loans payable, thereby affecting the fairness, reliability and completeness of presentation in the Financial Statements.

Cash Local Treasury amounting to ₱2.664 million remained undeposited as of December 31, 2019 contrary to the provisions of Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular 92-382, thus exposing government funds to possible loss or misapplication and rendered the existence and correctness of the account doubtful.

Cash advances amounting to ₱1.697 million remained outstanding as of December 31, 2019 contrary to Section 339 of Republic Act No. 7160 in relation with Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, thereby overstating the asset account and understating the corresponding expense accounts at the same time exposing funds to risk of misapplication.

Several liability accounts totaling to ₱1.579 million for CY 2019 remained dormant for more than three years, thus casted doubts over management assertions on completeness, existence, valuation and ownership; and affected the reliability and fairness of the concerned accounts and the related financial statements.

Differences between balances in the accounting records, bank confirmation replies and bank statements for Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account could not be validated due to non-preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements contrary to the provisions of Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011, thereby casting doubt on the correctness and reliability of the Cash and Cash Equivalents account presented in the Statement of Financial Position as of December 31, 2019.

Several cash advances for CY 2019 in the total amount of ₱11.984 million were granted without authorized specific purposes contrary to the provisions of Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003, thus casted doubts as to the validity of such expenses and exposed government funds to risks of misapplication.

The Municipality's available cash balance was insufficient to cover for its liabilities and obligations, including the unutilized balance of the 20% Development Fund as of December 31, 2019, thereby exposing Municipality to risks of not meeting its financial obligations which was not reflective of a sound fiscal administration contrary to Section 337 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 85 and 86 of PD No. 1445.

The Municipality incurred an excess of ₱14.886 million of Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses due to ineffective management of expenses and inappropriate charging of accounts, contrary to pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Procurement of goods and services that were paid out of the cash advances of the Disbursing Officer amounting to ₱448,195.00 were not supported with complete documentation contrary to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and were not done through the required procurement process as prescribed under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, thus propriety/validity of the transactions cannot be ascertained and obtaining the most advantageous prices was not ensured.

Fund Transfers for the implementation of projects under the Bottom Up Budgeting (BUB) and Recovery Assistance on Yolanda (RAY) Batch 3 programs from the DILG were not fully liquidated as of December 31, 2019, thereby exposing the Municipality to potential risk of non-compliance that may result to deferment of additional grants beneficial to the development of the Municipality's socio-economic condition.

The Municipality did not establish a Special Trust Fund for the unexpended Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and the non-submission of the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF contrary to Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121 and Item 5.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002, respectively, exposes the Fund to risk of misapplication and misuse.

Preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) was not in accordance with the guidelines, and gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of the GAD PPAs were not observed contrary to Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA, thus institutionalizing gender-responsive governance of the Municipality may not be attained.

The Municipality's Solid Waste Management programs, projects and activities were not properly implemented, thus the primary objective of Republic Act No. 9003 to protect the environment and public health may not be achieved.

Due to BIR amounting to ₱2.171 million remained unremitted as of December 31, 2019 contrary to the provisions of Sections 69 of PD No. 1445, BIR Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 and its amendments under BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, thus may result in the loss of revenue due the national government, misappropriation of funds, and incurrence of penalties/surcharges.

Due to GSIS amounting to ₱1.794 million remained unremitted as of December 31, 2019 contrary to the provisions of RA No. 8291 and its implementing rules and regulations, thus may exposed funds to risk of misappropriation, incurrence of interests and penalties/surcharges, and may deprive Municipal employees of the benefits therefrom.

The hiring of individuals on contracts of services amounting to ₱250,000.00 was undertaken without the required procurement process as prescribed under pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its implementing guidelines, and without complete and proper supporting documents contrary to COA Circular No. 2012-001, thus rendering the propriety and validity of the transactions doubtful.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality was not able to submit the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts within the prescribed period contrary to Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and the PPSAS Nos. 1 and 24, thus resulting to the delay in the audit of financial transactions and/ or accounts, as well as affecting the completeness and fairness of presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1 and 24, by submitting the complete set of financial statements, to include the SCBAA and supporting disclosure of an explanation of the reasons for material differences, if any, to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal Officials to:

a. Set more realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experiences and other relevant factors and statistics as aid in setting targets;

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality was not able to achieve its projected revenue for CY 2018 with a shortfall of P9,830,110.86; and revenue code and ordinances that have not been revised or updated since CY 1993 were used as basis for collections of taxes and fees, thereby casting doubts as to the propriety of taxes and fees imposed and the controls adopted in order to raise and collect additional revenue to support its programs, projects and activities that may benefit its constituents.

b. Undertake appropriate actions to facilitate proper execution of all laws and policies governing the revision of the Municipal Revenue Code and Ordinances; and

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality was not compliant to the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage and did not fully maximize the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF), hence may result to insufficient capability to act and respond in times of disasters/emergencies and the Municipality was not able to implement the PPAs pertaining to disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery in the amount of P2,484,875.95, thereby exposing the Municipality to risks during and after a disaster/ calamity and depriving the constituents of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, together with the other members of the LDRRM Council, comply with the disaster preparedness minimum standards on equipage to adequately prepare the Municipality when hazards/disasters strike.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
The Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory of all properties and equipment of the Municipality and to prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;
The Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designated to maintain and update the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for proper identification of all PPEs; and
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with the submitted RPCPPE in order to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant to analyze, reconcile and identify/trace the details of transactions debited or credited to Loans Payable Account and prepare adjusting entries to correct and present the appropriate balances in the financial statements;
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to request from the LBP - Iloilo Lending Center and DBP - Jaro Branch the appropriate supporting documents/records pertaining to the Municipality’s existing loans, and furnish the Municipal Accountant such copies of documents for verification and reconciliation of the differences/variances in the loan balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if any, to conform with the amounts provided by the Banks, and to present the correct amount of the Loans Payable; and
The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the current portions of the loans payable to the current liability in order to present properly the current and non-current liabilities in accordance with PPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to account for all undeposited collections and reconcile the records with that of the Accounting Office to properly reflect the amount of Cash Local Treasury in the Financial Statements;
The Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and
The Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure coordination between their offices for the timely and complete submission of all the required reports and necessary documents to facilitate the proper and appropriate recording of transactions involving the Cash Local Treasury account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs with complete supporting documents to the COA Auditor, and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to verify the balances and exert effort to locate the records and supporting documents to determine the validity of the claim and remit the exact amount to respective government agencies or make appropriate adjusting entries to achieve fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the accounting staff to recognize and record claims that are valid and supported with sufficient and appropriate documents, as well as, maintain subsidiary ledgers for proper monitoring that can support the balance presented in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) and concerned Municipal officials to adhere strictly to the provisions  of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular Nos. 2012-003 and 2012-001; and
The LCE to direct the Disbursing Officer to liquidate all her cash advances by submitting the liquidations reports and complete supporting documents to the Accounting Office; and in case the cash advance is not fully utilized, require refund of the excess amount evidenced by an official receipt.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive, as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to prepare the Annual Investment Plan containing priority development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only specific and identifiable PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets, attainable within the desired time frame, and consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; Moreover, the MDC and other heads of offices shall regularly monitor the progress of the projects, their timely, effective and efficient implementation, and the commitment from the concerned offices to implement the PPAs as planned; and
Ensure that the 20% DF is optimally and/or fully utilized in accordance with the guidelines to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We also recommended that the LCE direct the above-mentioned Municipal officials to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 by ensuring that expenses are properly charged and spent solely for the purpose intended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials and employees to:
Establish a Special Trust Fund for the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE;
Open a separate bank account for the LDRRMF where all the accumulated unutilized funds will be deposited; and
Prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in prescribed form as required in COA Circular No. 2012-002

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as Chairperson of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, direct the concerned officials to:
Ensure implementation of SWM PPAs under the 20% Development Fund; and
Utilize the fund transfer from the DENR-EMB Region VI in accordance with the specifications agreed upon in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the moneys withheld for the BIR and remit intact the full amounts thereto within the required period pursuant to the provisions of Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 and its amendments under BIR Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, otherwise penalties/surcharges on late remittances will be the personal liability of the concerned Municipal officials.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to determine the validity of the balance of the Due to BIR account as of January 1, 2019 and remit the amount actually withheld to the BIR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the moneys withheld for the GSIS and remit intact the full amounts thereto within the required period pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 8291 and its IRRs, otherwise the interests, penalties and surcharges on late remittances will be the personal liability of the concerned Municipal officials and employees.

We recommended that the Municipality shall comply with the proper procurement processes and documentation in the hiring of individual workers on contract of services pursuant to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to determine the proper charging and recording of the transactions to the appropriate accounts, and make the necessary corrections and/or adjustments.  Likewise, they submit the complete and required supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor.

Prepare and maintain separate cashbooks for all cash transactions and bank accounts;

Prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF), and submit to the Auditor, together with the individual RAAF of the collecting and liquidating officers, for monitoring of the receipts and issuances of accountable forms.

observations.recommendation:
Priority programs and projects under the Local Government Support Fund -Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (LGSF-ADM) were not fully implemented despite the availability of cash for immediate implementation in the total amount of P14,971,000.00 contrary to the guidelines provided in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3, thus depriving the Municipality’s constituents of the benefits that they could derive from the  socio-economic development projects/ outcomes; and copies of contracts and supporting documents were not furnished to the Auditor within five (5) working days from execution thereof contrary to COA Circular No. 2009-001, Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE direct the personnel responsible to undertake the procurement process and immediately implement the projects funded under the LGSF-ADM pursuant to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3, with strict compliance to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, as well as budgeting, accounting, and auditing rules and regulations in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those projects.

Recommendations:

The OIC-Municipal Accountant and/or Acting Municipal Treasurer to secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the bank statements after the end of each month for the timely preparation of the BRS.

Review/analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and
Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure coordination between their offices for the timely and complete submission of all the required reports and necessary documents to facilitate the proper and appropriate recording of transactions involving the Cash Local Treasury account.

We also recommended that the OIC–Municipal Treasurer require the collectors and other AOs to submit their individual RAAF at the end of the month as basis for the preparation and submission of the CRAAF.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare and submit/furnish copy of the monthly reports in the prescribed forms required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense and to the COA Auditor.

We recommended that the LCE implement strictly the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures. Exercise control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved and serially numbered DTTs, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels for audit purposes.  Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption shall likewise be submitted to the Auditor for verification to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.reason: Drivers Trip Tickets were prepared and attached to DVs but were not properly accomplished.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to assist her in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/documents.
observations.managementAction: There is only one staff handling the recording and preparation of reports for the GF and another one for both the SEF and TF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal officials and employees to coordinate and submit the documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for the timely recording of transactions in the books, and likewise submit same documents to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Existing differences /discrepancies were not yet reconciled between the balances of Loans-Payable account per books and per bank.

observations.managementAction: The amount of Loans Payable was not yet adjusted as of December 31, 2016 due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that at the end of the year, the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be transferred to a special trust fund solely for the purpose of supporting DRRM activities, and may be utilized for new expenditures upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local sanggunian and LCE upon recommendation of the MDRRMC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was not able to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.

Also, we recommended that the Municipality refrain from incurring additional PS expenses/costs until such time that the PS cap/ limitation is observed.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC–Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF to the Auditor not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: No CRAAF was submitted to the Auditor.

observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers were not able to submit the individual RAAF to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality prepare and submit/furnish copy of the monthly reports in the prescribed forms required in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 to the NDRRMC through the Office of the Civil Defense and to the COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The required reports were not submitted to the COA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, prepare the inventory reports of all PPE, and submit the same to the Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer.
We also recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled with the accounting records in order to present accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements.  Further, the Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer prepare and update the PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for proper identification of all PPE.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to request from the LBP - Iloilo Lending Center the appropriate supporting documents/records pertaining to the Municipality’s existing loans, and furnish copies to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for them to verify and reconcile the differences/ variances  in the loan balances, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if any, to conform with the amounts provided by the Bank, and to present the correct amount of the Loans Payable.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reclassify the loan amount which is due to be settled within twelve (12) months after the reporting date, to the current liability in order to present properly the current and non-current liabilities in accordance with PPSAS I.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly for SEF and TF to the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned, especially the OIC-Municipal Accountant, to obtain and follow-up their request from the LBP – Sara Branch, and all the necessary documents from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, to support the appropriate adjustments and reconciliations in the Cash in Bank accounts in order to properly reflect the true and accurate Cash in Bank balances in the Statement of Financial Position.
Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to assist her in the preparation of the BRS and the necessary supporting schedules.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to provide the List of NDs and Settlements with the corresponding amounts and record the unsettled disallowances under Receivables-Disallowances/ Charges in the books of accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the LCE direct the employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, by submitting copy of the MOA and other required documents to the Auditor for transactions involving fund transfers to properly evaluate the implementation of the projects.
We also recommended that the LCE coordinate with the NGAs concerned regarding the required terms and provisions to be incorporated in the MOA, specifically the timelines and/or schedules of implementation of the projects for proper monitoring of the progress of PPAs and evaluating the implementation of the projects and the utilization of funds for the purpose intended.
Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to request from the bank, copy of the credit advice and other documents pertaining to the amount credited to the Municipality’s account, as basis to identify and confirm with the source agencies the purpose of the fund transfer and request from the NGAs copies of MOA for the proper utilization and implementation of the projects. Likewise, it will be the basis for the proper accounting and reporting in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members of the MPDC, observe the guidelines of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating the PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only specific and identifiable PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets and achievable within the desired time frame.  By this, the Municipality may be able to maximize the purpose of the 20% DF which will have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Fund must be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to maximize the benefits for the constituents.
Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate bank account and subsidiary ledgers for the 20% DF for proper monitoring of the availability and utilization of the Fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees to take immediate actions on the matters listed below:
Prepare the GPB in accordance with the prescribed procedures to ensure that gender issues identified are the existing concerns/problems in the community, and see to it that the relevant LGU PPAs and GAD activities will address/respond to the gender issues identified.
Set-up and maintain a GAD Database that will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
Formulate GAD Codes or Ordinances that will support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
Prepare the GAD AR in accordance with the procedures and format provided in the JMC No. 2013-01 and support it with the necessary documents.
Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees, to liquidate and/or refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, no additional cash advance shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated pursuant to the aforecited provisions of law and regulations of COA.
We also recommended that the LCE only approve the grant of cash advance for a legally authorized specific purpose.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and/or OIC-Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1, 2 and 24, by submitting the complete set of financial statements, together with the accompanying notes and other supporting disclosure requirements to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality to disburse funds in accordance with the provisions of RA Nos. 5447 and 7160. The utilization of the SEF should focus in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education by public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Municipal officials to give priority for the implementation of allowable projects/activities included in the school board budget for the year.

We recommended that the LCE direct the department heads and project-in-charge concerned to adhere strictly to the terms and conditions provided in the MOA, contracts or other guidelines and provisions of the DILG pertaining to fund transfers, for prompt, efficient and effective implementation of projects and programs to avoid loss of opportunities on the part of the beneficiaries.
We also recommended that specific and attainable targets be set by both agreeing/contracting parties in order that the projects will be completed within the set timelines/implementation period; and that there will be commitment from the concerned departments to implement the project as planned and to regularly monitor the progress of the projects’ implementation.

We recommended that the LCE, as the Chairman of the MDRRMC, together with the other members of the Council, should ensure that the approved priority PPAs in the LDRRMFIP are implemented as planned to improve the Municipality’s disaster resilience.
We also recommended that the LCE direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant and MDRRMC-Deputized Coordinator to submit the monthly Report on Utilization of DRRMF to the Auditor using the prescribed format under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Further, we recommended that the LCE together with all the members of the MDRRMC and other personnel concerned to properly adhere with the provisions of JMC No. 2013-1 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.

We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the Reports of Checks Issued, all the paid DVs and its corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the COA Auditor.
We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted within 60 days from receipt of the AAR to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs and supporting documents for the months of February to May and August to December 2015 under the GF and January to December 2015 for the SEF and TF, and to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: There are still DVs and supporting documents not submitted by the Municipality.

Likewise, the reconciled inventory reports should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386.

The fund must be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE and other Municipal officials comply strictly to Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445 and Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160. We recommended that the LCE direct the employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001, and 96-010, by submitting copy of contracts/POs to the Auditor within five (5) working days from its execution, submitting the complete set of documents for transactions involving fund transfers and notifying the latter of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance of such deliveries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to construct a safe closure of the dumpsite and provide alternative means to control the wastes collected since the establishment of sanitary landfill is very costly.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit the DVs and supporting documents to the Auditor to ascertain the propriety, correctness and validity of transactions charged to the ESWMP.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS who shall spearhead the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR and observe strictly the provisions of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
